q.en: What group does the book say most ocean animals belong to?
q.zh: 书中说大多数海洋动物属于哪一类？

options.zh: 哺乳动物、鱼类或爬行动物

explain.en: The book says, 'Most of the animals in this book are mammals, fish, or reptiles.'
explain.zh: 书中说：“这本书里的大多数动物是哺乳动物、鱼类或爬行动物。”

options.en: Animals that hunt and eat other animals to survive
options.zh: 为了生存而猎食其他动物的动物
像磷虾那样的微小动物

explain.en: The glossary defines predators as 'animals that hunt and eat other animals to survive (p. 9).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 predators 定义为“为了生存而猎食其他动物的动物（第9页）”。

q.en: How does a blue whale catch its food?

options.en: It has baleen that traps krill after pushing water out with its tongue
options.zh: 它有鲸须，在用舌头把水推出后留住磷虾

options.en: It uses sharp teeth to bite fish
It catches fish with its tusks

explain.en: The book says the blue whale has baleen instead of teeth; 'After gulping large amounts of water, my tongue pushes the water out. Baleen traps my favorite food—tiny animals called krill—inside my mouth.'
explain.zh: 书中说蓝鲸没有牙齿而是有鲸须；“大口吞下大量水后，我的舌头把水推出来。鲸须把我最爱的食物——叫作磷虾的微小动物——留在嘴里。”

q.en: Which animal in the book is the slowest fish and latches its tail to coral?
q.zh: 书中哪种动物是最慢的鱼，并且把尾巴勾在珊瑚上？

explain.en: The sea horse says, 'I am the slowest fish. I can stay in one place by latching my tail onto a piece of coral or seagrass.'
explain.zh: 海马说：“我是最慢的鱼。我可以把尾巴勾在珊瑚或海草上停在原地。”

q.en: Why does the author include the last page about pollution?
q.zh: 作者为什么在最后加入关于污染的一页？

options.en: To encourage readers to keep oceans clean so animals survive
options.zh: 为了鼓励读者保持海洋清洁，让动物得以生存

options.en: To teach how to cook seafood

explain.en: The book concludes, 'Pollution and hunting are harmful to all these ocean animals, causing many to become endangered. By keeping our oceans clean, we can all work together to keep these animals alive.'
explain.zh: 书中总结道：“污染和捕猎对所有这些海洋动物都有害，导致许多物种濒临灭绝。通过保持海洋清洁，我们可以共同努力让这些动物存活下去。”

q.en: What does a hammerhead shark use its large head for?
q.zh: 双髻鲨用它的脑袋做什么？

options.en: To pin stingrays to the ocean floor

explain.en: The hammerhead shark says, 'I like to use my large head to pin stingrays, my favorite food, to the ocean floor.'
explain.zh: 双髻鲨说：“我喜欢用我的大脑袋把我最爱的食物——黄貂鱼——按在海底。”

options.en: Fastening or attaching to another object
options.zh: 固定或附着到另一物体上

explain.en: The glossary defines latching as 'fastening or attaching to another object (p. 12).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 latching 定义为“固定或附着到另一物体上（第12页）”。

q.en: How do octopus hide from predators and prey?
q.zh: 章鱼如何躲避捕食者和猎物？

options.en: By shooting black ink and changing the color of their skin
options.zh: 通过喷出黑色墨汁并改变皮肤颜色

explain.en: The octopus says, 'Shooting black ink and changing the color of my skin help me hide from both predators and prey.'
explain.zh: 章鱼说：“喷出黑色墨汁并改变皮肤的颜色，能帮助我躲避捕食者和猎物。”

q.en: How many eggs does a leatherback turtle lay?

explain.en: The leatherback turtle says, 'I lay between eighty and one hundred eggs on land by digging a nest in the beach sand.'
explain.zh: 棱皮龟说：“我在海滩沙子里挖巢，在陆地上产下八十到一百枚蛋。”

q.en: What makes the California sea lion special according to the book?
q.zh: 根据这本书，加利福尼亚海狮有什么特别之处？

options.en: It barks like a dog and is noisy, playful, and smart
options.zh: 它像狗一样叫，又吵又爱玩，而且聪明

options.en: It is the largest mammal ever
options.zh: 它是曾有过的最大的哺乳动物

options.en: It lives only in fresh water

explain.en: The California sea lion says, 'Known for barking like a dog, I am a California sea lion. Although people consider me noisy and playful, I am also smart.'
explain.zh: 加利福尼亚海狮说：“我以像狗一样叫而闻名。虽然人们觉得我又吵又爱玩，但我也很聪明。”

prompt.en: Choose one ocean animal and explain why it is interesting, using facts from the book.
prompt.zh: 选择一种海洋动物，并运用书中的事实解释它为什么有趣。

en: Name a specific animal from the book.
Include at least two facts from its description.
zh: 说出书中一种具体的动物。
包含它描述中至少两个事实。
